lever.description: eval_eig accumulated q_total via matrix_mul_complex (6 flops/element: re = ar*br - ai*bi, im = ar*bi + ai*br) even though q_total is provably purely real: it is initialized to the identity and only ever multiplied by qr_decomposition's Q, whose imaginary parts are exactly 0.0. Switched q_total to a Vec<f64> accumulated with the real matrix_mul (2 flops/element), wrapping to (re, 0.0) at the end. For zero-imaginary operands matrix_mul_complex's re = sum(ar*br - 0) equals matrix_mul's sum(ar*br) and its im = sum(0) = 0.0, so the eigenvectors are bit-for-bit identical. matrix_mul_complex is retained (allow(dead_code)) and unit-tested for the documented future complex-eigenvalue path.

benchmarks.method: rch Criterion, eval_primitive_multi(Eig, 48x48 f64); same-worker back-to-back; baseline = complex q_total (matrix_mul_complex), optimized = real q_total (matrix_mul). HEAD already includes the matmul-ikj (mcqr.24) and contiguous Gram-Schmidt (mcqr.25) eig wins.

after.tool: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'linalg/eig_48x48_f64'

benchmarks.note: 1.40x this lever (non-overlapping CIs). Cumulative across mcqr.24 (matmul ikj) + mcqr.25 (contiguous Gram-Schmidt) + this: the original eig (~61.5 ms two sessions ago) is now ~13.8 ms, ~4.4x. Worker was also less loaded this session, but the 1.40x is a same-worker back-to-back delta.

score.note: Algorithmic flop reduction (not just cache): eliminates guaranteed-zero complex arithmetic. Real, production (Eig primitive), bit-exact.

isomorphism_proof.floating_point: q_total and Q have imaginary parts identically 0.0. matrix_mul_complex computes re += ar*br - ai*bi = ar*br - 0.0 = ar*br (x - 0.0 == x in IEEE-754) and im += ar*bi + ai*br = 0.0; the real matrix_mul accumulates the same sum(ar*br) in the same ascending-k order and the final imaginary part is 0.0. Bit-for-bit identical eigenvectors.
isomorphism_proof.ordering: Accumulation order over k unchanged; eigenvalues come from t (untouched).
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.bit_identity_proof: Existing eig unit tests and the tensor_contraction/linalg oracle conformance tests (validated vs jnp) plus 1009/1009 fj-lax tests pass; eigenvectors are unchanged. No synthetic golden-bits file: the change is a provable no-op on the imaginary parts and the eig tests pin eigenvector correctness.
